entry.title: Transmit Purchase Order (PO) to Vendor via Email
entry.content:
Step 1: Define Communication Strategy

To ensure effective communication via Medium 5 (external sending), it's essential to maintain a communication strategy within the Customizing settings that support email. Without an appropriate communication strategy, communication with a partner cannot be established. By default, SAP provides the "CS01" communication strategy.

SPRO > Sales and Distribution > Basic Functions > Output Control > Determine Communication Strategy.

Step 2: Assign the Form and Processing routine to PO Output Type

SPRO > IMG > Material Management > Purchasing > Messages > Forms (Layout Sets) for

Messages > Assign Form and Output Program for Purchase Order

Output Type – NEU

Medium - 5 (External Send)

Program - SAPFM06P

Form Routine - ENTRY_NEU

Form - MEDRUCK

Step 3: Replacement of text symbols

 The replacement routine should be configured in the Customizing settings for the output type, on the 'General data' tab under 'Replacement of text symbols' in the detail screen.

SPRO > IMG > Materials Management > Purchasing > Messages > Output Control >

Message Types > Define Message Types for Purchase Order > Maintain Message Types for PO

Select Output Type "NEU" then click "Details"

Maintain the Replacement of text Symbols Program and FORM routine.

Program: SAPMM06E

FORM routine: TEXT_SYMBOL_REPLACE

Step 4: Maintain Email ID for supplier

You need to ensure that an email address is maintained in the vendor master under the Address View, system will only use the email address stored in the vendor master.

Also maintain your own user email address, this will be the sending email address.

System > User Profile > Own Data

Step 5: Maintain Conditions Records (Transaction: MN04)

Maintain the Condition Record for message Determination.

Select the Output type "NEU" and click "Key Combination"

Supplier: 31000

Partner Function: VN (Vendor)

Medium: 5 (External Send)

Dispatch Time: 4 (Send Immediately "When Saving the Application")

Select the Condition Record and Click "Communication".

Chose communication that we created (Refer step 1)

The "Cover Page Text" will serve as the email's subject. You can use variables (replacement parameters) in the cover page text, which must be enclosed in &. To utilize these replacement parameters, the configuration explained in Step 3 must be completed.

For example, if you want the title to be 'PO number: 100002', you should enter "PO number &EKKO-EBELN&".

Step 6: Create PO and Test the scenario

Create and release the PO

Click on messages to check the output

Output triggered with medium 5 to vendor 31000

Step 7: Check mail in SOST
If the correct SCOT/ SMTP configuration is maintained, emails will be sent to the supplier's email address stored in the Business Partner (BP) record

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite Trace Level is not working
entry.content:
Hello,

I confirm that the issue is not Browser related neither linked to CPI-Helper. In fact when using Cpi-helper to switch trace "on" we can confirm it has worked by checking the artifact status in "Manage Integration Content".

I agree with Walter, the procedure of undeploy (from Manage Integration Content page) then deploy from the iflow editor seems to do the trick.

I suppose it's indeed related to recent update and particularly the evolution of most used bricks like timer and groovy script (but this is only my personnal hypothesis).

entry.title: Re: SAC Data Action Advanced Formula - Calculation not re...
entry.content:
Hi @LavanyaPoluru 

In general, the RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures]="Committed_In_SAP",[d/Version]="public.Actual") can retrieve data from the actual version if the data exists. If it cannot retrieve the actual version data, the fact data on the actual version might be assigned to a "not in hierarchy" member. 

To read fact data assigned to the "not in hierarchy" member, you can add the following configuration to the data action script, which will enable access to the data.

  • CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY = [d/Account],[d/Entity]

Please add all dimensions that are listed on the dimension selection popup.

I hope this is helpful to you, and if I have misunderstood anything, please feel free to reach out.

Moonjun

            "DocumentCurrency": "USD",

        }
    ]
}

Regards

entry.title: SAP on IBM i: SAP Support Required PTF Lists have been updated
entry.content:
Last week, IBM i 7.5 TR5 was released along with Db2 for i PTF Group SF99950 Level 8, as well as IBM i 7.4 TR 11 along with Db2 for i PTF Group SF99704 Level 29. You can get a quick overview over the enhancements for IBM i 7.5 at https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/ibm-i-75-tr5-enhancements and for IBM i 7.4 at https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/ibm-i-74-tr11-enhancements. A general entry point for the various Technology Refreshes with their enhancements can be found at https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/node/1119129.

The SAP Support Required PTF Lists, formerly known as Information APARs, have been updated to include these new levels. Together with the Technology Refreshes and the Db2 for i PTF groups, the cumulative PTF packages and the High Impact/Pervasive PTF groups were updated. For IBM i 7.3 and IBM i 7.2, only the High Impact/Pervasive PTF groups were updated. A few individual PTFs that are not yet part of a PTF group or cumulative PTF package were added as well.

The PTF lists can be found at the following links:

IBM i 7.5: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-75
IBM i 7.4: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-74
IBM i 7.3: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-73
IBM i 7.2: https://www.ibm.com/support/pages/sap-support-required-ptf-list-ibm-i-72

If you want to download the PTF lists to your SAP systems with the help of a program, take a look at our sample program and blog entry from July 18th, 2022.

Note: If your current PTF level is older than June 2024, you may be affected by the security changes that we described in our blog article from June 18, 2024: SAP on IBM i: New PTF Groups trigger IBM i administrator actions. To prevent or overcome the potential authority failures, you can either follow the instructions in the blog article or upgrade your SAP Host Agent to release 7.22, patch level 65 or higher.

We recommend that you apply the current PTF levels for preventive maintenance whenever you are performing your next scheduled maintenance.

entry.title: How to check the status of SAP Cloud ALM service key
entry.content:
I am currently trying to connect Cloud ALM to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

I am following the steps with reference to the following guide:
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/setup-managed-services/setup-s4hana-cloud.html?anchorId=section_copy

I understand that as a prerequisite, I need to obtain an SAP Cloud ALM service key.

However, I don't know how to check if the service key has already been obtained.
I would like to know how to check.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to check the status of SAP Cloud ALM service key
entry.content:
The service key can be check in two ways:

1. In BTP Cockpit

  1. Log on to the SAP BTP cockpit.
  2. Click on your SAP Cloud ALM global account, then on your SAP Cloud ALM sub-account.
  3. In the navigation panel, choose Services > Instances and Subscriptions.
  4. Under Instances click on the relevant instance of the service SAP Cloud ALM API
  5. In the area that opens, choose Service Keys.
  6. You see your service key in JSON format. The key includes the application URL, service URL, client ID, and client secret.
  7. Download this service key and save it for later

2. In Cloud ALM - Landscape Management, click on Configuration -> section "SAP Cloud ALM Service Key".

You can get more information in SAP Expert Portal of Retrieve SAP Cloud ALM Service Key:
https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/integration-monitoring/calm-connect.html

Best regards,
Ellie
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Datasphere News in December
entry.content:
What a year it has been. Wow! 2024 was packed with great events, lots of new features and enhancements to extend existing features. Explore the latest updates in our community news blogs and enjoy watching my top feature highlights for 2024 on YouTube below.

 

SAP Datasphere 2024 highlights in a nutshell

What a great ride it has been in 2024 with SAP Datasphere!
Let me share a few of my personal highlights.

 

184 innovations were delivered according to What's New in SAP Datasphere on SAP Help. This is just the tip of the iceberg, and we know there is always a demand for more features and even quicker delivery. Thousands of code lines were written, tested, deployed and finally delivered as part of the solution. Kudos 👏 to all you developers, UX designers, fellow product managers to make this happen. You guys rock 💪

20k+ views of monthly top feature videos on YouTube about SAP Datasphere. I started this monthly series in January 2024 and did not expect this kind of success. Thanks for bearing with me and all the great feedback I got ❤️

11,967 attendees live at all SAP Datasphere sessions at SAP TechEd. This year we only had a virtual TechEd event. Personally, I love the onsite events and the interaction with customers, partners, consultants at our both and also during the lectures and hands-on sessions. Nevertheless, the studio interaction with my partners in crime Nikola Braukmüller and Dominik Kurz was lots of fun. Blessed to have such great teammates 🙏

 

My top features in 2024

Picking my top features for all of 2024 was not easy and here is my top feature list in no particular order.

Folder Support

In January we delivered the long-awaited support for folders inside the data builder and repository of SAP Datasphere. The feature allows to organize your content in folders within a space.

 

Nested & Shared Task Chains

February already covered my next favorite feature which are nested task chains, so the ability to use other task chains within a task chain. These meta chains help you coordinate many other task chains and since November you can share them across spaces to enable global task chains.

 

Confluent Kafka

Event streaming is a great way of data exchange between systems. Confluent is one of our open data ecosystem partners and this year we delivered Confluent Kafka as a target earlier in the year, and since November we also support Confluent Kafka as a source in replication flows to enable end-to-end scenarios.

 

Column Level Lineage

As a data modeler and data integrator it is always good to understand the flow of data on a field level. With the column level lineage, the impact and lineage analysis is now enabled to get one level deeper and allows you to select one or more columns in your analyzed object and show their sources and the columns that they impact.

 

Elastic Compute Nodes

The elastic compute nodes (ECN) enable you to get additional compute resources on-demand – while keeping your baseline compute resources untouched. You can spin up ECN’s according to your own schedule to help you expand elasticity during peak consumption scenarios and decommission them during regular operations.

 

Object store (Controlled availability)

With our final release of the year, we delivered the object store in a controlled availability. The object store provides an inbound layer to stage data in a cost-effective way. You can use replication flows to replicate data to local tables (file) and you can optionally further prepare the data with Apache Spark-based transformation flows. You can then share the tables to standard spaces, where they can be used as sources for flows, views, and analytic models (SAP Note 3525760, 3538038).

 

 

And more feature highlights of 2024

There are so many more great features which we delivered in 2024, so in addition to my 2024 top features, I pulled together the 2024 feature highlights in all different areas of SAP Datasphere for you. Enjoy the ride!

 

Data Modelling

 

Catalog & CLI

 

Data Integration

 

Spaces, Elasticity, Transport and more

 

My top blogs in December

This second blog in the SAP BW series by @SandyTran offers great value to our big community of BW experts looking for options on how to implement certain functions well known from SAP BW and BW/4HANA inside of SAP Datasphere. Especially interesting are the dynamic filtering options.
➡️  SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started (Part 2)

@Sefan_Lindersexplains how you can access two SAP Datasphere spaces using the same SQL user and gives you a step-by-step guide how you create such a user
➡️  Create a SQL user with access to multiple spaces in SAP Datasphere

 

Find more information and related blog posts on the topic page for SAP Datasphere. You will find further product information on our Community with various subpages about Business Content, the SAP BW Bridge as well as content for Best Practices & Troubleshooting. Also check out the new support content for SAP Datasphere on SAP Help for troubleshooting and analysis guides, how-to guides, technical details, and more.

Find out how to unleash the power of your business data with SAP’s free learning content on SAP Datasphere. It’s designed to help you enrich your data projects, simplify the data landscape, and make the most out of your investment. Check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on  SAP Learning site.

entry.content:

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – [Updated] Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori
entry.content:
As you may already know, SAP recommends deploying SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA with your SAP Fiori Front-End Server in embedded mode (embedded FES) , as outlined in the SAP Fiori Deployment Options and System Landscape Recommendations. 

Embedded FES reduces server cost and complexity and simplifies ongoing operations as you grow your usage of SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA innovations.

If you began your SAP S/4HANA journey with a SAP Fiori Front-End Server in standalone mode (standalone FES), you might be asking:
How do I transition from standalone FES to embedded FES deployment?

The Regional Implementation Group for SAP S/4HANA has worked with many customers to help them move to SAP Fiori Front-End Server Embedded deployment. Based on these experiences, we have created the first version of the document in 2019 as an easy-to-follow, step-by-step guide. This guide outlined the migration process, explaining both the technical setup and how to ensure a smooth experience for users.

We’re excited to announce our updated Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guide based on SAP S/4HANA release 2023.

This new version provides more streamlined steps, updated tools and further recommendations to help you successfully move from standalone FES to embedded FES deployment.

Moving from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Why do you need this guide?

This guide is essential for SAP S/4HANA customers who are looking to move from a standalone FES to an embedded FES deployment. It provides clear, step-by-step instructions to make the transition smooth, covering both technical and practical aspects of the migration.

Whether you are upgrading your SAP S/4HANA system or aligning with SAP's latest architectural recommendations, this guide ensures you have everything you need i.e. from planning, installation, and configuration to post-migration testing and optimization. It helps you:

  • Understand the benefits of moving to embedded FES deployment.
  • Know the right time to make the shift.
  • Navigate technical prerequisites, sizing, and system considerations.
  • Simplify the migration process with actionable steps, tools, and examples.
  • Ensure a seamless experience for users by resolving common issues.
  • Minimize disruptions by retaining user personalization and role assignments.
  • Gain tips on how to enhance usability and performance

By following this guide, you can align with SAP best practices to unlock the full potential of SAP Fiori embedded FES deployment.

 

What's in it / What's new?

  • Enhance performance with tips for optimizing launchpad features, OData services, and app configurations

    • This includes reviewing and improving performance for SAP GUI Apps, SAP Fiori Apps, SAP Fiori Launchpad etc. and best practices to adjust and transport launchpad configuration parameters
  • Why and how to use Clean Core SAP Fiori administration tools to perform the migration
    • Clean core tools like Launchpad Content Manager, Launchpad Application Manager, Manage Launchpad Pages, Manage Launchpad Spaces provide enhanced capabilities compared to classical tools like Launchpad Designer. 
  • Structured 6-Step Content Migration
    • Comprehensive steps to migrate roles, catalogs, spaces and pages.
  • User Personalization Retention
    • Tools like /UIF/MIGRATE_FES_PERSO to migrate homepages, private views, and UI changes.
  • Multi-Client Activation
    • Detailed guidance on enabling and configuring new clients post-migration.
  • Improved Testing and Troubleshooting
    • Expanded scenarios for UAT, error handling using App Support, and validation tools.
  • Performance and usability enhancements
    • Parameters for faster navigation, optimized search, and stateful SAP GUI containers.
  • Web Dispatcher Guidance
    • Steps to switch from Standalone to Embedded without changing end-user URLs.

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: Festival of People Analytics 2025: Celebrating The Value Of People Data This January
entry.content:
I had a chance to chat with Nigel Dias, managing director of 3n Strategy, and the chair and founder of the HR Analytics ThinkTank, to discuss this year’s FOPA which starts on Tuesday, January 28th 2025. 

All SuccessFactors customers and SAP staff can attend for free, if they select ‘Special Guest Ticket’ and use the code SAP100 at the Checkout Page. To register to attend, please go here. 

Andrea Domhardt (SAP): Nigel, it’s great to be chatting with you ahead of this year’s Festival of People Analytics. I’m excited to dive into what’s new for 2025. Let’s start with the big picture—what’s the story behind this year’s Festival? 

Nigel Dias (3n Strategy): Thanks, Andrea. This year’s Festival builds on our core mission of celebrating how organisations are unlocking value with people data using SAP SuccessFactors. In 2025, we’re placing a stronger focus on showcasing real-world impact—how analytics with SAP SuccessFactors is not just about data but about creating meaningful outcomes for HR teams, employees, and businesses. We’ve designed the Festival as a collaborative platform, bringing together SAP customers, HR leaders, and analytics experts to share stories, innovations, and how they’ve overcome data challenges. It’s a chance to celebrate achievements and help attendees discover practical ways to enhance their own analytics journey.

Andrea: This year’s tagline, "Celebrating the Value of People Data", can you share more about what inspired it?

Nigel: Absolutely. At its heart, people analytics is about using data to improve decision-making. Over the years, we’ve seen how organisations are using tools like SAP Analytics Cloud, report stories within SAP SuccessFactors, and SAP Datasphere to drive adoption and deliver measurable value. The tagline reflects that maturity. It’s about recognising the incredible potential of these technologies while celebrating the tangible outcomes they deliver—whether it’s improving workforce retention, streamlining operations, or supporting strategic HR planning. 

Andrea: What can attendees expect from the sessions? 

Nigel: We’re thrilled to have an agenda packed with actionable insights. Over two days, attendees will hear from leading organisations about how they’re driving data adoption and achieving business value. 

Day 1 focuses on strategy and adoption—how senior executives and HR teams align data initiatives with broader business goals. Day 2 dives into tools and technology, featuring case studies on SAP Datasphere, report stories in SAP SuccessFactors, and how organisations are leveraging SAP Analytics Cloud to inform their decisions. We’re also bringing back the much-loved “Ask Me Anything” panel with SAP SuccessFactors’ product team. It’s a rare chance for users to ask questions and hear directly from the experts. 

Andrea: It’s so valuable for customers to engage directly with the people shaping the tools they use every day. Speaking of customer stories, what kind of case studies can attendees look forward to this year? 

Nigel: This year, we have brilliant contributions from organisations like Mott MacDonald and Groupe Bel. They’ll share how they’ve used SAP SuccessFactors to achieve results like better workforce planning and more effective succession planning reporting. One session I’m particularly excited about is on adoption. It’s a topic many organisations struggle with, and hearing how others have successfully built data-driven cultures will be invaluable. 

Andrea: It sounds like there’s something for everyone. Before we wrap up, why should SAP customers and prospects make time to attend the Festival this year? 

Nigel: The Festival is a one-stop shop for anyone looking to accelerate their people analytics journey. It’s not just about learning what’s possible—it’s about building connections, finding inspiration, and walking away with practical ideas to take back to your organisation. If you’re looking to stay ahead in this fast-evolving space and understanding how SAP SuccessFactors enables best-in-class people analytics, there’s no better place to be. And for those who can’t join live, recordings will ensure no one misses out.

entry.title: Why Delaying System Modernization is Your Biggest Business Risk in 2025?
entry.content:
Have you heard the popular story of the “Midnight Blizzard” attack on Microsoft earlier this year? During that time, the world’s most sophisticated technology company fell victim to password spray attack due to its legacy application that didn’t have multifactor authentication enabled. Now can you imagine the threat that legacy systems could bring to even very guarded business environments?  

As a COO who has navigated digital transformation since the mid-2010s, I've seen the same story play out repeatedly for several organizations. Therefore, the prevalence of legacy systems in every company is alarming now.  

While the investment in modernization may seem expensive, the cost of inaction is far greater than that. Can you imagine businesses spend nearly $3 million last year just to update or maintain their legacy systems? 

However, the demands of new-age space are completely different – damaging hack is no longer an issue you should consider because legacy systems cost your business in various strategic forms, from employee churn to declining profits.  

Above all, while you're postponing modernization, citing budget constraints or operational stability, your competitors are quietly building their digital empires. McKinsey's latest research also reveals that companies who modernized their legacy systems in the past two years are experiencing 43% higher operational efficiency and a 31% increase in market share. 

In today's landscape, where AI and cloud technologies are reshaping entire industries overnight, running on legacy systems isn't just risky – it's like trying to compete in F1 with a vintage car. Sure, it might be reliable and familiar, but you're not just losing the race; you're not even qualifying. 

So, I'll share the unfiltered reality of what's at stake when you delay modernization, backed by real-world lessons I have learnt from my experience. More importantly, I'll show you why 2024 is your critical decision point, and what you can do about it before it's too late. 

Here’s What You Pay for Ignoring On-time Modernization. 

Being in the market for over 24 years, I can tell you that the decision to delay modernization services of legacy system is not just a technical over-sight; it’s a strategic misstep with far-reaching consequences. Here’s how:   

  1. Forgone Profits: A Direct Hit to Your Bottom Line 

Legacy systems are a financial burden that siphons off resources that could be better utilized in digital transformation or somewhere else. Moreover, organizations will allocate 40% of their IT budgets solely to maintaining technical debt by 2025. Now, imagine how this reality will impact every organization with legacy applications. When you’re spending most of your budget on outdated systems, you’re effectively locking away funds that could drive innovation and growth. That’s why, I always emphasize - The longer you wait to modernize, the more you risk falling behind competitors who are agile and ready to seize market opportunities. 

  1. Lost Productivity: Inefficiencies That Suppress Growth 

Legacy systems act as a drag on productivity. I’ve seen teams waste countless hours navigating clunky interfaces and dealing with slow response times. Research indicates that employees spend up to 20% of their time working around these inefficient or outdated systems. This inefficiency not only leads to employee burnout but also missed deadlines and lost opportunities. In a world where speed and agility are highly prioritized, clinging to legacy systems is akin to running a marathon in heavy boots. 

  1. Increase Opportunity Cost: Choking Your Competitive Edge 

The inability to innovate can be fatal for modern-day businesses that live in speed-first environment. And legacy systems often lack the flexibility needed to integrate new technologies or adapt to changing market demands. This stagnation can prevent your organization from launching new products or services that meet customer needs. Even studies have shown that companies that modernize their systems can achieve economic gains ranging from 20% to 50% of their cost base. Ultimately, it clearly defines how ignoring modernization means you’re not just missing out on innovation; you’re risking your relevance in the marketplace. 

  1. Customer Exit: Disappointment Breeds Defection 

Customer expectations are evolving rapidly, and legacy systems can lead to a disjointed customer experience. When clients encounter delays or errors due to outdated technology, they will not hesitate to take their business elsewhere. In fact, studies show that 32% of customers will walk away from a brand after just one bad experience. If your systems can’t keep pace with customer demands, you risk alienating your most valuable asset: your customers. Remember, Modernization is not just upgrading technology; it involves maintaining trust and loyalty in your brand. 

  1. Security Breaches: A Risk No-one Can Afford 

The security risks associated with legacy systems are profound. In an era where data breaches can cost millions and irreparably damage reputations, relying on outdated technology is a gamble you cannot afford. Have you ever realized that the global average cost of a data breach reaches approx. $4.88 million in 2024, the highest average on record? The reality is that legacy systems often lack the robust security features that is required to protect sensitive data. This way, delaying modernization puts your entire organization at risk, exposing you to potential financial and reputational ruin. 

  1. High Maintenance Costs: The Money Pit That Drain Resources 

Finally, let’s address the elephant in the room: maintenance costs. Still, organizations are spending 60-80% of their IT budgets on maintaining legacy systems. This is not just a drain on resources; it’s a barrier to growth. This leaves a mere 20% for development and modernization, stifling innovation and progress. By modernizing your systems, you can redirect those funds toward initiatives that drive value and growth. 

Closing Thoughts  

I hope now you are pretty much aware of the fact that - the strategic risks of ignoring legacy system modernization are not just abstract but real and immediate. So, it’s high time to view legacy system modernization as a strategic imperative for futureproofing your business.  

Remember, a true modernization will not just update your software but secure your company's future in a world where digital agility isn't just an advantage; it's a prerequisite for survival.  

So, embrace the future with modernization and seize your position in the new-age market before it’s too late!

entry.title: Integrating SAP HR with Business Partner (BP)
entry.content:
Scope of the Blog:

This guide outlines the key steps to integrate SAP HR with Business Partner (BP), ensuring seamless data synchronization and enhancing organizational efficiency. The process involves activating the necessary business functions, configuring data synchronization parameters, and maintaining critical settings like address and identification types. Number range intervals are aligned for both personnel numbers and BP groupings to ensure consistency.

Key Steps Include:

Activate Integration: Enable HR-BP synchronization for personnel numbers, organization units, and employee data.
Data Synchronization: Set parameters like PNUMB and PSUBG for number range assignment and synchronization.
Logging/Error Analysis: Enable tracking for error identification and resolution.
Maintain Address & Identification Types: Ensure accurate mapping for employee IDs and addresses.
Number Range Configuration: Align employee and BP number ranges for a unified setup.
Final Synchronization: Run reports like /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE to complete integration and validate data.

Pro Tips:

  • Maintain consistent number range names and lengths (2 alphanumeric digits).
  • Use troubleshooting tools like SLG1 and RPU46CX_CENTRAL_PERSON_ONLINE for error resolution.

Step by Step Configuration:

1️⃣Activate Business Function:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Personnel Management > Organizational Management > Integration > Integration with SAP Business Partner > Activate Integration

  • HRALX – HRAC: Maintain "X" to sync BP & Personnel Numbers.
  • HRLAX – OBOPN: Maintain "ON" for Org Unit & BP auto-sync.
  • HRALX – PBPHR: Maintain "ON" to sync employee changes with BP.

2️⃣Data Synchronization: Persons - Activate Business Partners:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Personnel Management > Integration with SAP Business Partner > Data Synchronization

  • Set parameters like PNUMB and PSUBG for BP synchronization and number range assignment.
  • HRALX – PNUMB: Set to "3" for BP ID = Personnel Number.
  • HRALX – PSUBG: Choose appropriate BP number range (internal/external).

3️⃣Logging/Error Analysis (Optional):

Path: SPRO > IMG > Personnel Management > Organizational Management > Integration > Integration with SAP Business Partner > Data Synchronization: Persons - Activate Logging/Error Analysis for Data Synchronization

  • Enable logging for error tracking and resolution.

4️⃣Maintain BP Address Types:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Cross-Application Components > SAP Business Partner > Business Partner > Basic Settings > Address Determination > Define Address Types

  • Check address types like HCM001 for private addresses and HCM003 for main employment

5️⃣Maintain Identification Types:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Cross-Application Components > SAP Business Partner > Business Partner > Basic Settings > Identification Numbers > Define Identification Types

  • Ensure below identification types are properly configured.
    • HCM001 Employee ID
    • HCM028 Internal Personnel Number
    • HCM030 Employee ID (Service Performer)
    • HCM031 Employee ID (Freelancer)
    • HCM032 Personnel Number (Main Employment)
    • HCM033 Personnel Number (Employment)

6️⃣Maintain Number Range Intervals for Personnel Numbers:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Personnel Management > Personnel Administration > Basic Settings > Maintain Number Range Intervals.

7️⃣Determine Defaults for Number Ranges:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Personnel Management > Personnel Administration > Basic Settings > Determine defaults for number ranges.

  • Assign Personal number range no to country.

Pro Tip:

  • Create Number range No. and BP Grouping name as same with length of 2 alphanumeric digits because of HRALX/PSUBG have length of 2 alphanumeric digits.
  • Maintain both Employee (RP_PREL) and BP (BU_PARTNER) number range interval as same and flagged as external for BP number range.

8️⃣Define Number Range for Employee BP:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Cross-Application Components > SAP Business Partner > Business Partner > Basic Settings > Number Ranges and Groupings > Define Number Ranges

9️⃣Assign Number Ranges to Groupings:

Path: SPRO > IMG > Cross-Application Components > SAP Business Partner > Business Partner > Basic Settings > Number Ranges and Groupings > Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges

  • Define Grouping with 2 alphanumeric digits and assign Number range No.

🔟Final Setup & Data Synchronization:

Run the report /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE for employees,

  • This report takes PERNR as input.
  • Check if in IEMPLOYEE view (in SE16 transaction) full name column has data for the employee.
  • In IEMPLOYEE view find the record by first name and last name and also check in this view Employee column is filled.

Check for any errors in transaction SLG1.

Pro Tip: Use RPU46CX_CENTRAL_PERSON_ONLINE to fix CP-P relation issues.

✅Follow these steps to ensure seamless synchronization of employee data and business partners, enhancing HR operations and organizational efficiency.

entry.title: Re: Operator Alert Triggering and CIT creation
entry.content:
Hi Eduardo,

From your explanation, I gather that the rating operation in CC completes successfully (otherwise, the counters wouldn't be updated, and you'd get an error somewhere).
One way of diagnosing this issue would be to add the problematic fields in the user alerts. If the expected values make it to your notification's recipient, then it means that the fields are properly computed by the pricing logic.
In that case, you'll have to focus on the item mapping, in order to understand why the values are computed but not sent with the item.

Additionally (also within CC), you may enable the "CIF" debug traces in the bulkloader and the rater (and also the "JCO" debug traces for the bulkloader, if you want).
The "CIF" traces will show the items that are generated by CC (and therefore expected to be sent to CI afterwards).
The "JCO" traces show what CC actually sends to CI over the JCo RFC interface (which will normally reflect exactly the contents of the CIF traces).

Feel free to ask for clarifications if needed.
Best regards.

François
SAP Convergent Charging Support

entry.title: Re: Is it a good idea to learn SAP Security GRC without an IT background? Any suggestions?
entry.content:
Hi KSR,

  1. Programming Knowledge: No, SAP Security GRC doesn’t require programming skills.
  2. Salary and Demand: Average salary is INR 8-15 LPA, and there’s steady demand.
  3. Opportunities for Freshers: Yes, there are opportunities for freshers with relevant training.
  4. Training Institutes: Good training institutes are available in Hyderabad, and online platforms like SAP Learning Hub can help.
  5. Certification: Certification helps but isn’t mandatory; it can cost between INR 30,000+.

Best regards,
